label: Prostate
quick_take: Prostate is the PSA-era cancer: the vol VI → X inverted-U in every Western registry is essentially a screening wave. The deeper stories are (1) the persistent US Black male excess (ratios of 8–14×, barely narrowed since vol IV), (2) the catch-up in East-Asian men once PSA arrived, and (3) a clean Algerian U-shape that argues for pure ascertainment change.

findings.title: US Black men sit at the top of every volume
findings.text: Peak prostate ASRs: Detroit Black 202.0 (vol VIII), SEER Black 185.4 (vol VIII), Atlanta Black 181.5, Connecticut Black 181.4, LA Black 176.3. The vol-X Black peak (Atlanta 206.7) is the highest site-specific ASR for any cancer in US data.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US Black men sit at the top of every volume

findings.title: Asian catch-up is dramatic
findings.text: Singapore Chinese men 0.81 → 33.81 (+4074 %), Shanghai 1.32 → 16.77, Osaka 4.55 → 40.50 (+790 %), Miyagi 5.41 → 39.95. Half the rise is PSA uptake; half is obesity + diet.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Asian catch-up is dramatic

findings.title: Newfoundland and Labrador men: inverted-U from 20 (vol I) to 95.5 (vol XI) and back to 75.8 (vol XII)
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Newfoundland and Labrador men: inverted-U from 20 (vol I) to 95.5 (vol XI) and back to 75.8 (vol XII)

findings.title: Spain and Israel-Arab men show the same inverted-U
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Spain and Israel-Arab men show the same inverted-U

findings.title: Algeria Sétif U-shape: 0.28 (vol VI) → 0.20 (vol VII) → 7.74 (vol XI)
findings.text: Pure ascertainment pattern — the early volumes missed cases, then an active registry + PSA filled in the gap.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Algeria Sétif U-shape: 0.28 (vol VI) → 0.20 (vol VII) → 7.74 (vol XI)

findings.title: Uganda Kyadondo: 5.74 → 53.0 (+823 %)
findings.text: African rise across the full CI5 window; not a screening artefact because PSA is much less prevalent. This is a genuine burden increase, plausibly driven by ageing population + westernising diet.

findings.title: US Black-to-White/Asian ratios 8–14× across volumes
findings.text: and barely narrowing by vol XII (Black 149 vs Hispanic 15 = 9.7×). One of the most persistent ethnic gradients in oncology.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — US Black-to-White/Asian ratios 8–14× across volumes

findings.text: PSA screening entered clinical practice around 1987 (US) and over the early 1990s in Europe; any volume VII–IX rise in Western registries is at least partly attributable to prevalence-pool detection rather than underlying incidence.
No formal CI5 warning, but the direction of the "inverted-U" peaks aligns tightly with registry-specific PSA-screening uptake timing.
The Black male prostate excess across five decades is one of the most important persistent health disparities in US cancer data — a long-window CI5 chart would reframe it.
Uganda's rise (+823 %) alongside other African registries (Zimbabwe Harare, South Africa) is a genuine non-screening story that deserves its own paper.
Contrast Japan's pre-PSA (vol I–V) low rates, the post-PSA climb (VI–X), and the partial plateau (XI–XII) to reveal the "true" underlying incidence.
